instruction: Create a Blueprint that prints Hello World when the game starts.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_HelloWorld
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Hello World"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_HelloWorld
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Hello World"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_HelloWorld
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Hello World"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint for an actor that continuously rotates at 90 degrees per second on the Yaw axis.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_RotatingActor
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: MakeRotator [X=0, Y=0, Z=90]
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat
NODE n4: AddActorLocalRotation

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.B [Rotator]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.DeltaRotation [Rotator]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_RotatingActor
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: MakeRotator [X=0, Y=0, Z=90]
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat
NODE n4: AddActorLocalRotation

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.B [Rotator]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.DeltaRotation [Rotator]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_RotatingActor
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: MakeRotator [X=0, Y=0, Z=90]
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat
NODE n4: AddActorLocalRotation

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.B [Rotator]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.DeltaRotation [Rotator]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that prints Entered when another actor overlaps it and prints Exited when the overlap ends.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_OverlapZone
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Entered"]
NODE n3: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Exited"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_OverlapZone
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Entered"]
NODE n3: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Exited"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_OverlapZone
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Entered"]
NODE n3: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Exited"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint with a boolean variable IsActive. On BeginPlay, check if IsActive is true. If true, print Active. If false, print Inactive.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_BranchExample
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsActive: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Branch
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Active"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Inactive"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.True -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.False -> n4.Execute

DATA n1.IsActive -> n2.Condition [Bool]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_BranchExample
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsActive: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsActive]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Active"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Inactive"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_BranchExample
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsActive: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsActive]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Active"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Inactive"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that waits 3 seconds after the game starts, then destroys itself.

NODE n2: Delay [Duration=3.0]

EXEC n2.Completed -> n3.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay uses a Sequence node to first print Step 1, then print Step 2, then print Step 3.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SequenceExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Step 1"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Step 2"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Step 3"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_0 -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_1 -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_2 -> n5.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SequenceExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Step 1"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Step 2"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Step 3"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_0 -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_1 -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_2 -> n5.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SequenceExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Step 1"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Step 2"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Step 3"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_0 -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_1 -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.Then_2 -> n5.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint that toggles an actor's visibility each time the player presses the Interact input action using a FlipFlop node.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ToggleVisibility
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Interact"]
NODE n2: FlipFlop
NODE n3: SetActorHiddenInGame [NewHidden=false]
NODE n4: SetActorHiddenInGame [NewHidden=true]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n4.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ToggleVisibility
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Interact"]
NODE n2: FlipFlop
NODE n3: SetActorHiddenInGame [bNewHidden=false]
NODE n4: SetActorHiddenInGame [bNewHidden=true]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n4.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ToggleVisibility
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Interact"]
NODE n2: FlipFlop
NODE n3: SetActorHiddenInGame [bNewHidden=false]
NODE n4: SetActorHiddenInGame [bNewHidden=true]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n4.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on overlap, casts the other actor to a Character class. If the cast succeeds, print Player Found.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CastExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Player Found"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n3.Execute

DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CastExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Player Found"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n3.Execute

DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CastExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Player Found"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n3.Execute

DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]

instruction: Create a Blueprint with an array of Actors called TargetActors. On BeginPlay, loop through each actor in the array and destroy it.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DestroyAll
PARENT: Actor

VAR TargetActors: Array = None

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: ForEachLoop
NODE n3: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.LoopBody -> n3.Execute

DATA n1.TargetActors -> n2.Array [Array]
DATA n2.ArrayElement -> n3.Target [Actor]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DestroyAll
PARENT: Actor
VAR TargetActors: Array<Actor>
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=TargetActors]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: DestroyActor
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n4.Execute
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.Target [Actor]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DestroyAll
PARENT: Actor
VAR TargetActors: Array<Actor>
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=TargetActors]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: DestroyActor
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n4.Execute
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.Target [Actor]

instruction: Create a Blueprint with a Gate node. On BeginPlay, open the gate. On Tick, pass through the gate and print Ticking. On pressing the Close input action, close the gate.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: Event_Tick
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Ticking"]
NODE n5: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Close"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n3.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Close
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: Event_Tick
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Ticking"]
NODE n5: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Close"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n3.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Close
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateExample
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: Event_Tick
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Ticking"]
NODE n5: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Close"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n3.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Close

NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Activated"]

EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint with an integer variable Score starting at 0. On overlap, add 10 to the score and print the new score.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ScoreCounter
PARENT: Actor

VAR Score: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVariable [VarName="Score"]
NODE n3: AddInt
NODE n4: SetVariable [VarName="Score"]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Int]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.InString [String]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ScoreCounter
PARENT: Actor

VAR Score: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n3: AddInt [B=10]
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ScoreCounter
PARENT: Actor

VAR Score: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n3: AddInt [B=10]
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that spawns a projectile actor at the player's location when the Fire input action is pressed.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ProjectileSpawner
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Fire"]
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: GetActorRotation
NODE n4: SpawnActor [ActorClass="BP_Projectile"]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n4.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n4.SpawnLocation [Vector]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.SpawnRotation [Rotator]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SpawnProjectile
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Fire"]
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: SpawnActorFromClass [ActorClass=BP_Projectile]
EXEC n1.Pressed -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.SpawnTransform [Vector]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SpawnProjectile
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Fire"]
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: SpawnActorFromClass [ActorClass=BP_Projectile]
EXEC n1.Pressed -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.SpawnTransform [Vector]
